title: man pages render with lots of empty lines
body:
### Issue Description

In most instances where a single empty line is expected, the output man page has two lines.

This makes reading through the page require continuous scrolling, even on a large, portrait mode screen. It also simply looks wrong.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `man podman-run`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:

```
podman-run(1)               General Commands Manual              podman-run(1)

NAME
       podman-run - Run a command in a new container


SYNOPSIS
       podman run [options] image [command [arg ...]]


       podman container run [options] image [command [arg ...]]


DESCRIPTION
       Run a process in a new container. podman run starts a process with its
       own file system, its own networking, and its own isolated process tree.
       The image which starts the process may define defaults related to the
       process that will be run in the container, the networking to expose,
       and more, but podman run gives final control to the operator or
       administrator who starts the container from the image. For that reason
       podman run has more options than any other Podman command.


       If the image is not already loaded then podman run will pull the image,
       and all image dependencies, from the repository in the same way running
       podman pull image, before it starts the container from that image.


       Several files will be automatically created within the container. These
       include /etc/hosts, /etc/hostname, and /etc/resolv.conf to manage
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
podman-run(1)               General Commands Manual              podman-run(1)

NAME
       podman-run - Run a command in a new container

SYNOPSIS
       podman run [options] image [command [arg ...]]
       podman container run [options] image [command [arg ...]]

DESCRIPTION
       Run a process in a new container. podman run starts a process with its
       own file system, its own networking, and its own isolated process tree.
       The image which starts the process may define defaults related to the
       process that will be run in the container, the networking to expose,
       and more, but podman run gives final control to the operator or
       administrator who starts the container from the image. For that reason
       podman run has more options than any other Podman command.

       If the image is not already loaded then podman run will pull the image,
       and all image dependencies, from the repository in the same way running
       podman pull image, before it starts the container from that image.

       Several files will be automatically created within the container. These
       include /etc/hosts, /etc/hostname, and /etc/resolv.conf to manage
       networking.  These will be based on the host's version of the files,
       though they can be customized with options (for example, --dns will
       override the host's DNS servers in the created resolv.conf).
       Additionally, a container environment file is created in each container
       to indicate to programs they are running in a container. This file is
       located at /run/.containerenv (or /var/run/.containerenv for FreeBSD
```

(note the extra content that fits on screen).

### podman info output

```yaml
n/a
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

This is most likely an issue in the pipeline that converts md.in into man(7).

### Additional information

I had a look at `hack/markdown-preprocess`, and I don't think the issue lies there.

comments.body: I don't see this on Fedora. Are you using packaged manpages from a distro, or building them yourself?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
As there never was a reply closing, I don't see the issue either on Fedora or debian:sid
Pages are distro packages. They're build just calling make: https://gitlab.alpinelinux.org/alpine/aports/-/blob/053449ea04f6c96457c4098038a3f1736caa9c3c/community/podman/APKBUILD#L95-103

I tried to double-check Fedora, but I'm not sure how to install the documentation for `podman-run` there. The following didn't work:

```con
> podman run -it --rm fedora
# yum install man podman
…
# man podman-run
No manual entry for podman-run
```
`dnf in -y --setopt='tsflags=' man podman` notice tsflag here, by default the dnf config (/etc/dnf/dnf.conf) in the container image disables doc downloads with `tsflags=nodocs`
Thanks, I checked on Fedora and the man pages are rendered fine. I'll report this downstream on Alpine.

~@Luap99 Could you try if you can reproduce the issue when using `mandoc` (instead of `man-db`)?~

Edit: Looks like ether a `go-md2man` or `mandoc` issue so no need to debug this here further.

The man pages contain empty lines, which is non-standards.

`man-db` ignores this, but `mandoc` renders the extra line.

Looks like (at least part of) this is being fixed in md2man: https://github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man/issues/47
